headline: Bakun Dam Displaced 10,000 Orang Ulu with Broken Promises
context: Twenty-five years after resettlement began, a 2025 SUHAKAM inquiry found that Bakun Dam displaced communities at Sungai Asap still lack promised land titles and infrastructure.

cards.big: Bakun Dam was Malaysia's largest infrastructure project at RM7.4B.
cards.sub: 10,000 Orang Ulu were resettled to Sungai Asap with promises of 3-acre farms and modern housing.

cards.big: Only 23% of promised land titles have been issued after 25 years.
cards.sub: Without titles, residents cannot access agricultural loans or sell property. They are tenants on their own resettlement land.

cards.big: Bakun generates 2,400MW but Sarawak uses only 1,200MW domestically.
cards.sub: The submarine cable to Peninsular Malaysia was never built. Excess capacity sits unused.

cards.big: Sungai Asap has no hospital — the nearest is 4 hours away in Bintulu.
cards.sub: The original resettlement plan included a hospital, secondary school, and commercial centre. None were built.

cards.big: When the dam generates power but the displaced still lack electricity titles and hospitals, who benefited?
Bakun is a case study in broken compacts. The state's obligation to displaced communities does not expire when the project is completed.
